catalysts.catalyst_details: Expected to be in-line to slightly better than consensus due to strong AI demand, stabilization in non-AI semi business, and strong VMware momentum.
catalysts.catalyst_dates: Apr quarter (F2Q25) results, reported around May 30, 2025
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Broadcom is set to report Apr quarter (F2Q25) earnings Thursday, and we continued to see a strong demand profile for its AI products (custom ASICs and networking solutions), stabilization in the non-AI semi business (e.g., enterprise, server/storage, broadband, wireless) and continued VMware revenue synergy unlock. As a result, we expect Apr-qtr revenue/earnings/FCF to be in-line to slightly better vs our/consensus expectations. • Expect in-line to slightly better Apr-Qtr results on continued strong AI demand, stabilization in non-AI semi business, and continued strong VMWare momentum. We believe that Apr quarter revenue/earnings/FCF were in-line to slightly better than ours/consensus expectations on AI strength and continued solid software renewals in its VMware business.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Expected to be up 5-7% Q/Q growth, in-line to slightly better than consensus, closer to J.P. Morgan's $16.1B estimate, led by AI demand strength.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Off of a higher Apr-rev Qtr base, we see Jul-Qtr revenue guidance up 5-7% Q/Q growth, in-line to slightly better than consensus expectation and closer to our above-Street estimates of $16.1B led by AI demand strength (we estimate AI revenues of $5B+ vs consensus of $4.8B). • Expect Jul-Qtr guide to be in-line to slightly better vs expectation on continued AI strength (custom ASICs and networking) and gradually improving trends in non-AI semi business….start of the ramp of its 3nm TPU ASIC programs... Off of a higher Apr-rev Qtr base, we see Jul-Qtr revenue guidance in-line to slightly better than consensus and closer to our above-Street estimates of $16.1B.

catalysts.catalyst_topic: Google TPU v6 3nm ASIC chip production ramp
catalysts.catalyst_details: Start of the production ramp for Google TPU v6 3nm ASIC chip, expected to be a strong growth driver for Jul-Qtr and remainder of the year, and into FY26. Estimated to drive >$15B in lifetime revenues and be the most powerful custom XPU AI accelerator.
catalysts.catalyst_dates: Jul-Qtr and remainder of FY25, into FY26
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: This is driven by continued strong demand in AI networking and start of the Google TPU v6 3nm ASIC chip production ramp which should be a strong growth driver for the Jul-Qtr and remainder of the year. We estimate the Google TPUv6 3nm (2 compute die, 8 stacks HBM3e) should drive >$15B in lifetime revenues and will be the most powerful custom XPU AI accelerator in the world. The strength in AI is led by the start of the production ramp of its TPU v6 3nm ASIC chip program with Google and we expect that to be a strong growth driver through the 2H of the year and into FY26. The TPU v6 3nm training chip is in production ramp and should contribute to AI revenues in the Jul-Qtr and 2H revenue growth driver.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Expect the start of the next-gen Tomahawk 6 (3nm) ramp to commence.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: On the networking front, we see continued strong demand for its AI networking products (Tomahawk 5/Jericho 3 AI) and expect the start of the next-gen Tomahawk 6 (3nm) ramp to commence in the 2H of this year. In total, we expect the team to drive~$19-$20B (up 60% Y/Y) in AI revenues in FY25 on continued strong ramp of Google’s TPU AI processor, continued ramp up of its other ASIC programs ( e.g. Meta/ByteDance) and sustained demand for its AI networking products (Tomahawk 5/Jericho 3 AI) and start of Tomahawk 6 ramp.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Continued ramp up of other ASIC programs for customers like Meta and ByteDance, contributing to FY25 AI revenues.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: In total, we expect the team to drive~$19-$20B (up 60% Y/Y) in AI revenues in FY25 on continued strong ramp of Google’s TPU AI processor, continued ramp up of its other ASIC programs ( e.g. Meta/ByteDance) and sustained demand for its AI networking products (Tomahawk 5/Jericho 3 AI) and start of Tomahawk 6 ramp. • Strong AI growth driven by lead customer’s next-gen XPU program ramp and continued strong networking demand - we expect $19-$20B in AI Revenues in FY25 (up ~60% Y/Y). We expect the team to drive ~$19-$20B in AI (ASIC+networking) revenues this year (up from $12.2B last year) led by its lead XPU customer combined with incremental revenue contribution from its other AI ASIC customers (e.g. Meta/Bytedance) combined with continued strong AI networking revenues.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Gradual improvement in non-AI semi businesses on improving cyclical trends with potential revenue upside in the wireless segment.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: The non-AI semi businesses should gradually improve on improving cyclical trends with potential revenue upside in the wireless segment. • Expect Jul-Qtr guide to be in-line to slightly better vs expectation on continued AI strength (custom ASICs and networking) and gradually improving trends in non-AI semi business….start of the ramp of its 3nm TPU ASIC programs... We expect the team to drive $5B+ in its AI business (vs consensus of $4.8B) combined with gradual improvements in its non-AI semi businesses.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Strong momentum in its VMware business on strong software renewals with conversion/upsell to its VCF full stack solution with higher ASPs with large corporate customers.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: In the software infrastructure business, we continue to see strong momentum in its VMware business on strong software renewals (with conversion/upsell to its VCF full stack solution with higher ASPs) with its large corporate customers. • Expect in-line to slightly better Apr-Qtr results on continued strong AI demand, stabilization in non-AI semi business, and continued strong VMWare momentum. We believe that Apr quarter revenue/earnings/FCF were in-line to slightly better than ours/consensus expectations on AI strength and continued solid software renewals in its VMware business.

catalysts.catalyst_topic: AI ASIC chip tape-out for OpenAI and Softbank/ARM
catalysts.catalyst_details: Broadcom is making good progress and should be on track to tape-out new AI ASIC chips for OpenAI and Softbank/ARM.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We believe the team continues to make good progress at OpenAI and Softbank/ARM and should be on track to tape-out these new AI ASIC chips in 2H25.

catalysts.catalyst_details: The Meta 3nm MTIA program is on track to ramp, expected to contribute to a strong FY26 AI revenue growth profile.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: The Meta 3nm MTIA program is on track to ramp in 1H CY26 and should contribute to a strong FY26 AI revenue growth profile.

catalysts.catalyst_topic: FCF growth, dividend growth, balance sheet de-leveraging
catalysts.catalyst_details: Expect double-digit% FCF growth, supportive of continued strong dividend growth over the next several years, and continued balance sheet de-leveraging to reduce interest expense and drive earnings power.
catalysts.catalyst_dates: Next several years (for dividend growth), ongoing
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: • Strong FCF growth and capital return outlook. We expect the team to drive double-digit% FCF growth, which will be supportive of continued strong dividend growth over the next several years. In addition, we expect the team to continue to de-lever its balance sheet, which should help reduce its interest expense burden and drive earnings power.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Strong demand for AI products (custom ASICs and networking solutions).
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: we continued to see a strong demand profile for its AI products (custom ASICs and networking solutions)

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Stabilization and gradual improvement in non-AI semiconductor businesses.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: stabilization in the non-AI semi business (e.g., enterprise, server/storage, broadband, wireless)

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Continued strong momentum and revenue synergy unlock from VMware.
Anticipated in-line to slightly better Apr-Qtr results and Jul-Qtr guidance.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: As a result, we expect Apr-qtr revenue/earnings/FCF to be in-line to slightly better vs our/consensus expectations. Off of a higher Apr-rev Qtr base, we see Jul-Qtr revenue guidance up 5-7% Q/Q growth, in-line to slightly better than consensus expectation and closer to our above-Street estimates of $16.1B led by AI demand strength (we estimate AI revenues of $5B+ vs consensus of $4.8B).

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Significant growth driver from Google TPU v6 3nm ASIC chip production ramp, expected to generate >$15B in lifetime revenues.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: This is driven by continued strong demand in AI networking and start of the Google TPU v6 3nm ASIC chip production ramp which should be a strong growth driver for the Jul-Qtr and remainder of the year. We estimate the Google TPUv6 3nm (2 compute die, 8 stacks HBM3e) should drive >$15B in lifetime revenues and will be the most powerful custom XPU AI accelerator in the world.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Expected ~$19-$20B in AI revenues in FY25 (up 60% Y/Y) from various ASIC programs and networking products.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: In total, we expect the team to drive~$19-$20B (up 60% Y/Y) in AI revenues in FY25 on continued strong ramp of Google’s TPU AI processor, continued ramp up of its other ASIC programs ( e.g. Meta/ByteDance) and sustained demand for its AI networking products (Tomahawk 5/Jericho 3 AI) and start of Tomahawk 6 ramp.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Broadcom's leadership position in AI infrastructure.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Broadcom remains our overall top pick in our semiconductor coverage given their exposure to AI infrastructure spending trends (#2 global AI semi supplier and # 1 custom chip AI ASIC supplier and #1 cloud/AI networking switch/routing chip supplier)

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Diversified end market exposure and best-in-class margins.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: combined with their diversified end market exposure and best in-class gross, operation, and free cash flow margins

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Strong Free Cash Flow (FCF) growth and capital return outlook, supporting dividend growth and balance sheet de-leveraging.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Strong FCF growth and capital return outlook. We expect the team to drive double-digit% FCF growth, which will be supportive of continued strong dividend growth over the next several years. In addition, we expect the team to continue to de-lever its balance sheet, which should help reduce its interest expense burden and drive earnings power.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Progress on new AI ASIC chips with OpenAI and Softbank/ARM, and Meta 3nm MTIA program ramp for FY26.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We believe the team continues to make good progress at OpenAI and Softbank/ARM and should be on track to tape-out these new AI ASIC chips in 2H25. The Meta 3nm MTIA program is on track to ramp in 1H CY26 and should contribute to a strong FY26 AI revenue growth profile.

thesis_analysis.central_thesis: Broadcom is poised for significant growth and strong financial performance, driven primarily by its leadership in AI infrastructure, the successful integration and momentum of VMware, and a stabilizing non-AI semiconductor business, all contributing to robust revenue, earnings, and free cash flow generation.

sub_claims.claim: Strong and accelerating growth in AI-related semiconductor business.

evidence_list.content: we continued to see a strong demand profile for its AI products (custom ASICs and networking solutions)
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst observation based on market insights and company interactions.

evidence_list.content: AI demand strength (we estimate AI revenues of $5B+ vs consensus of $4.8B)
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Specific quantitative estimate provided by J.P. Morgan, exceeding consensus.

evidence_list.content: driven by continued strong demand in AI networking and start of the Google TPU v6 3nm ASIC chip production ramp which should be a strong growth driver for the Jul-Qtr and remainder of the year.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst forecast identifying key growth catalysts for the near to medium term.

evidence_list.content: We estimate the Google TPUv6 3nm (2 compute die, 8 stacks HBM3e) should drive >$15B in lifetime revenues and will be the most powerful custom XPU AI accelerator in the world.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Quantitative estimate from J.P. Morgan for lifetime revenues, combined with a qualitative assessment of market leadership.

evidence_list.content: On the networking front, we see continued strong demand for its AI networking products (Tomahawk 5/Jericho 3 AI) and expect the start of the next-gen Tomahawk 6 (3nm) ramp to commence in the 2H of this year.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst observation of current demand and forecast for future product ramps.

evidence_list.content: In total, we expect the team to drive~$19-$20B (up 60% Y/Y) in AI revenues in FY25 on continued strong ramp of Google’s TPU AI processor, continued ramp up of its other ASIC programs ( e.g. Meta/ByteDance) and sustained demand for its AI networking products (Tomahawk 5/Jericho 3 AI) and start of Tomahawk 6 ramp.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Specific quantitative revenue and growth estimates for FY25 from J.P. Morgan, based on multiple AI initiatives.

evidence_list.content: Broadcom remains our overall top pick in our semiconductor coverage given their exposure to AI infrastructure spending trends (#2 global AI semi supplier and # 1 custom chip AI ASIC supplier and #1 cloud/AI networking switch/routing chip supplier)
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst's strong recommendation ('top pick') supported by specific market ranking claims, which are presented as objective facts.

evidence_list.content: We believe the team continues to make good progress at OpenAI and Softbank/ARM and should be on track to tape-out these new AI ASIC chips in 2H25.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst's assessment of progress and future milestones for new AI ASIC chip development.

evidence_list.content: The Meta 3nm MTIA program is on track to ramp in 1H CY26 and should contribute to a strong FY26 AI revenue growth profile.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst's assessment of a key customer program's progress and its expected future revenue contribution.

sub_claims.claim: Robust performance and synergy realization from the VMware software infrastructure business.

evidence_list.content: continued VMware revenue synergy unlock.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst observation of ongoing positive developments from the VMware acquisition.

evidence_list.content: strong momentum in its VMware business on strong software renewals (with conversion/upsell to its VCF full stack solution with higher ASPs) with its large corporate customers.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst observation detailing specific drivers of VMware's strong performance.

evidence_list.content: continued solid software renewals in its VMware business.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst observation reinforcing the stability and strength of VMware's recurring revenue.

evidence_list.content: aggressive synergy/value creation in the software business.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst's qualitative assessment of management's strategy and its positive impact.

sub_claims.claim: Stabilization and gradual improvement in non-AI semiconductor segments.

evidence_list.content: stabilization in the non-AI semi business (e.g., enterprise, server/storage, broadband, wireless)
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst observation indicating a halt in decline and potential for recovery in these segments.

evidence_list.content: The non-AI semi businesses should gradually improve on improving cyclical trends with potential revenue upside in the wireless segment.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst forecast based on expected cyclical improvements and specific segment potential.

evidence_list.content: stabilizing/improving demand trends in telecom, server/storage, and broadband, and slightly better order trends in wireless.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst observation providing specific examples of improving demand across various non-AI segments.

evidence_list.content: gradual improvements in its non-AI semi businesses.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst's general forecast for positive trajectory in these business areas.

sub_claims.claim: Strong financial outlook and shareholder returns.

evidence_list.content: expect Apr-qtr revenue/earnings/FCF to be in-line to slightly better vs our/consensus expectations.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst forecast for near-term financial results, indicating positive performance relative to expectations.

evidence_list.content: Off of a higher Apr-rev Qtr base, we see Jul-Qtr revenue guidance in-line to slightly better than consensus and closer to our above-Street estimates of $16.1B.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Specific quantitative revenue guidance estimate from J.P. Morgan, exceeding consensus.

evidence_list.content: Strong FCF growth and capital return outlook.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst's qualitative assessment of the company's financial strength and commitment to shareholder returns.

evidence_list.content: We expect the team to drive double-digit% FCF growth, which will be supportive of continued strong dividend growth over the next several years.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Quantitative forecast for FCF growth and its implication for dividend policy, provided by J.P. Morgan.

evidence_list.content: In addition, we expect the team to continue to de-lever its balance sheet, which should help reduce its interest expense burden and drive earnings power.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Forecasted balance sheet management strategy and its expected positive impact on earnings, provided by J.P. Morgan.

evidence_list.content: Best in-class gross, operation, and free cash flow margins.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst's qualitative assessment of Broadcom's superior profitability metrics.

evidence_list.content: Price Target (Dec-25):$250.00
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Specific, quantifiable price target set by J.P. Morgan for the stock.

evidence_list.content: Our Dec 2025 price target of $250 assumes that A VGO trades at 31x our FY25 estimated exit run-rate earnings power of ~$8.10 per share (=4 * $2.03).
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Detailed, quantifiable methodology and assumptions underpinning J.P. Morgan's price target.

evidence_list.content: The multiple reflects sustainable long-term revenue growth, strong operating model leverage, and robust free cash flow generation.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst's qualitative justification for the chosen valuation multiple, linking it to fundamental strengths.

evidence_list.content: Adj. EPS growth 39.4% (FY25E)
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Specific quantitative growth estimate for Adjusted EPS for FY25 from J.P. Morgan.

evidence_list.content: Revenue Growth Y/Y (%) 23.5% (FY25E)
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Specific quantitative growth estimate for Revenue for FY25 from J.P. Morgan.

evidence_list.content: EBITDA Growth Y/Y (%) 28.8% (FY25E)
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Specific quantitative growth estimate for EBITDA for FY25 from J.P. Morgan.
Specific quantitative estimate for Free Cash Flow to Firm for FY25 from J.P. Morgan.

evidence_list.content: Gross margin 77.5% (FY25E)
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Specific quantitative estimate for Gross Margin for FY25 from J.P. Morgan.

evidence_list.content: EBITDA margin 70.9% (FY25E)
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Specific quantitative estimate for EBITDA Margin for FY25 from J.P. Morgan.

evidence_list.content: Net debt/Equity 0.4 (FY25E)
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Specific quantitative estimate for Net Debt to Equity ratio for FY25 from J.P. Morgan, indicating improved leverage.

sub_claims.claim: Broadcom's strong market position and diversified portfolio.

evidence_list.content: Broadcom remains our overall top pick in our semiconductor coverage given their exposure to AI infrastructure spending trends (#2 global AI semi supplier and # 1 custom chip AI ASIC supplier and #1 cloud/AI networking switch/routing chip supplier) combined with their diversified end market exposure and best in-class gross, operation, and free cash flow margins
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst's strong recommendation ('top pick') supported by specific market ranking claims, which are presented as objective facts, and qualitative assessment of diversified exposure and superior margins.

evidence_list.content: We see Broadcom as a leader in wireless, data center networking, AI/deep learning ASICs, storage, and infrastructure silicon, hardware, software with broad-based exposure to positive trends in these end markets.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst's assessment of Broadcom's leadership across multiple key technology segments.

evidence_list.content: Broadcom is a technology infrastructure powerhouse with unmatched scale and technology capabilities in the industry, securing its leadership positions in a diverse set of end markets.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Analyst's qualitative assessment highlighting Broadcom's strategic importance and competitive advantages.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Unexpected negative shocks to the global economy or any regional end market could lead to changes in demand for Broadcom's products, potentially impacting estimates and ratings.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Broadcom’s products are sold into a wide range of end markets and are sold to thousands of customers worldwide. If the global economy or any regional end market suffers unexpected negative shocks that lead to changes in demand for Broadcom’s products, we may reassess our estimates and our Overweight rating.
